channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: A future NATO conflict will be fought against civilian targets
item.description: Jacek Siewiera stated a future NATO conflict will target civilians. Jacek Siewiera: a future NATO conflict will be fought against civilian targets Resilience Media.

item.title: U.S. Blockade Will Last ‘As Long as It Takes,’ Hegseth Says
item.description: The Israeli military extended its truce with Lebanon by three weeks, despite striking Hezbollah targets on Friday. The cease-fire aims to end the war in Iran. The Israeli military said it had struck Hezbollah targets on Friday, but its truce with Lebanon appeared to be holding.

item.title: U.S. Blockade Around Strait of Hormuz Will Last ‘As Long as It Takes,’ Hegseth Says
item.description: The US blockade around the Strait of Hormuz will last as long as necessary, according to Hegseth. Iran War Live Updates: U. S. Blockade Around Strait of Hormuz Will Last ‘As Long as It Takes,’ Hegseth Says The New York Times.

item.title: Hegseth says U.S. not anxious for deal with Iran; naval blockade of Iran 'going global'
item.description: Hegseth said the US is not anxious for a deal with Iran and the naval blockade is expanding. Iran's FM Araghchi likely to reach Pakistan tonight for talks with U. S., report says; Indian seafarer safe as vessel in Iranian custody moved to safer zone.

item.title: Top Iran diplomat set to travel to Pakistan for talks on ceasefire with US
item.description: The talks come amid ongoing tensions between Iran and the US. Live updates: Iran's top diplomat is expected to travel to Pakistan by this weekend for talks about a ceasefire with the US AP News.

item.title: U.S. and Iran remain locked in Strait of Hormuz standoff, Hezbollah rejects Lebanon ceasefire
item.description: The US and Iran remain in a standoff in the Strait of Hormuz, with Hezbollah rejecting a Lebanon ceasefire. Live Updates: U. S. and Iran remain locked in Strait of Hormuz standoff, Hezbollah rejects Lebanon ceasefire CBS News.

item.title: UK position on Falklands will not change, No 10 says after leaked Pentagon memo
item.description: The UK's position on the Falklands remains unchanged, according to No 10, after a leaked Pentagon memo suggested reassessing US support.

item.title: Netanyahu says he was successfully treated for prostate cancer
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu, 76, was treated for prostate cancer. He delayed releasing his medical report to prevent Iranian propaganda. Netanyahu, 76, said that he had delayed the release of his annual medical report by two months to prevent Iran from spreading "false propaganda against Israel".

item.title: Pro-Palestinian activists break into suspected Elbit Systems drone factory
item.description: Pro-Palestine activists broke into a UK factory suspected of making drones for Israel's military. Pro-Palestine activists have broken into a UK factory where they said drones were being made for Israel’s military.

item.title: Negotiations that enable Israel’s land-grabs
item.description: Negotiations since Oslo have coincided with Israel's settlement expansion and Palestinian land loss. Since Oslo, negotiations have coincided with illegal settlement expansion and the continued loss of Palestinian land. Negotiations that enable Israel’s land-grabs.

item.title: Iranian FM Araghchi to visit Pakistan in step towards US talks resumption
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi will visit Pakistan to discuss resuming US talks. As Pakistan hosts US-Iran talks, twin-city residents face road closures, halted courts and growing economic strain. Iranian FM Araghchi to visit Pakistan in step towards US talks resumption.

item.title: Benfica player handed six-game ban for homophobic conduct towards Real star Vinicius Junior
item.description: Benfica's Gianluca Prestianni was banned for six matches for homophobic conduct towards Vinicius Junior. Benfica's Gianluca Prestianni has been banned for six matches by UEFA for homophobic conduct towards Real Madrid's Vinicius Junior during a Champions League match, with three of those suspended for a two-year period.

item.title: Pentagon Chief Hegseth Says Iran Has Chance To Make 'Good Deal'
item.description: Iran may make a deal according to Pentagon Chief Hegseth. US-Iran War News Live Update:. Pentagon Chief Hegseth Says Iran Has Chance To Make 'Good Deal'.

item.title: Hopes rise for renewed US-Iran talks as Tehran’s foreign minister reported to be heading to Pakistan
item.description: Tehran's foreign minister is heading to Pakistan for talks. Middle East crisis live: Hopes rise for renewed US-Iran talks as Tehran’s foreign minister reported to be heading to Pakistan The Guardian.

item.title: South Africa's top envoy to Ghana summoned over attacks on foreigners
item.description: South Africa's envoy to Ghana was summoned over attacks. A Ghanaian national was confronted by South Africans in a viral clip and questioned about his immigration status. South Africa's top envoy to Ghana summoned over attacks on foreigners.

item.title: Iranian Delegation May Arrive In Pakistan Tonight For Talks With US: Report
item.description: An Iranian delegation may arrive in Pakistan for US talks. Multiple Pakistani media reported that a US logistics and security team is already present in Islamabad. Iranian Delegation May Arrive In Pakistan Tonight For Talks With US: Report.

item.title: Oil Markets Reprice Risk as Geopolitics Overtake Fundamentals
item.description: Geopolitics are affecting oil markets and crude oil prices. Oil Markets Reprice Risk as Geopolitics Overtake Fundamentals Crude Oil Prices Today | OilPrice. com.

item.title: Iran's foreign minister to head to Islamabad, raising hopes of negotiations with US
item.description: Iran's foreign minister will visit Islamabad, raising hopes for US-Iran talks. A US logistics and security team is already in place in Pakistan, according to two government sources. The visit may pave the way for negotiations between the two countries.

item.title: What Would It Mean to Defend One Another Without NATO?
item.description: European Union nations have a little-known obligation to defend each other. However, experts warn this obligation is no substitute for NATO membership, and its effectiveness remains uncertain. European Union nations have a little-known obligation to protect one another.

item.title: US mulls expelling Spain from Nato for failing to back war on Iran
item.description: The US considers expelling Spain from Nato for not backing the Iran war. US mulls expelling Spain from Nato for failing to back war on Iran Middle East Eye.
